title: State Police sgt. accused of drunk driving is now a defendant in family’s lawsuit over fatal crash
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Sgt. Scott Quigley faces a wrongful death lawsuit from Angelo Schettino's family, seeking $25 million in damages.
Quigley, accused of drunk driving in 2023, initially only received a ticket for the crash involving Schettino.
Middlesex investigation found State Police omitted key crash details, failing to notify prosecutors of Quigley's alleged intoxication.

title: Here are the Mass. beaches closed Wednesday as temps hit the high 80s
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Seven beaches in Massachusetts are closed due to high bacteria levels, affecting Quincy, Ashby, Plymouth, and Shutesbury.
Quincy beaches are shut because of bacteria, while reasons for Ashby, Plymouth, and Shutesbury closures were not specified.
Beach closures generally last one to two days to allow bacteria levels to return to safe standards.

title: Stephen Colbert’s best ‘Late Show’ moments
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Stephen Colbert concluded his 11-year run on 'The Late Show' with a star-studded finale featuring Paul McCartney.
Colbert's tenure included standout moments like Liv Tyler's reenactment of 'Lord of the Rings' and Joe Biden discussing grief.
David Letterman reunited with Colbert in 2026 for a memorable segment of comedic furniture tossing on 'The Late Show.'

title: Red Sox have had little to show for their ‘Hail Mary’ coaching change
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: The Boston Red Sox are nine games under .500 at 22-31, struggling despite recent coaching changes.
Chad Tracy, interim manager, replaced Alex Cora a month ago with little improvement in the team's performance.
Red Sox struggle with offensive production and bullpen issues, contributing to a poor 8-18 home record.

title: Recent homes sales in Greater Boston (May 27)
author: The Boston Globe
bullets: Abington saw sales of a 1951 ranch for $455,000 and a condo for $305,000.
An Andover Colonial on Porter Road sold for $3,005,000, built in 1968 with 7 rooms and 3 baths.
Boston's 288 Commonwealth Ave. condo sold for $9,750,000, featuring 3 bedrooms and 5 baths.

title: 4 Patriots roster positional battles to follow during OTAs
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Patriots' OTAs spotlight offensive line depth, with rookie Caleb Lomu competing for key backup roles.
Rhamondre Stevenson and TreVeyon Henderson lead running backs; Jam Miller and Lan Larison vie for third spot.
Linebacker corps depth a concern; K.J. Britt and Namdi Obiazor target roles post off-season changes.

title: Former Stoughton cop accused of killing Sandra Birchmore denied bail pending trial
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Farwell, an ex-Stoughton police officer, denied bail for the alleged 2021 murder of Sandra Birchmore.
Judge M. Page Kelley cited strong evidence and potential flight risk in denying bail for Farwell's upcoming trial.
Farwell allegedly strangled Birchmore, staged it as suicide, and destroyed evidence; trial starts October 5.

title: Joe Mazzulla named NBA Coach of the Year for 2025-26 season
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Joe Mazzulla named NBA Coach of the Year for 2025-26, first Celtics coach to receive the award since 1980.
Mazzulla led the Celtics to a 56-26 record despite the absence of star player Jayson Tatum for most of the season.
Boston Celtics were eliminated by the Philadelphia 76ers in the first round despite holding a 3-1 series lead.

title: New Hampshire mother and son found dead in suspected murder-suicide
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Eileen Shevelin, 78, and her son Thomas Wallace, 57, were found dead in Dover, NH home.
Wallace appeared to suffer a fatal gunshot wound; officials suspect a murder-suicide.
Authorities state there is no public threat; autopsies scheduled in Concord, NH.

title: Red Sox get good news on Roman Anthony, Garrett Crochet’s injuries
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Roman Anthony is recovering from a sprained finger, improving after taking 12-15 dry swings with less pain.
Garrett Crochet threw an inning of live batting practice with improved velocity, showing progress from shoulder inflammation.
Red Sox interim manager Chad Tracy indicated both Anthony and Crochet are showing promising signs in their injury recoveries.
